## What to Look For When Buying Cheap Cars in Norwich

Even when you're on a budget, it's smart to do your homework. Here are a few pointers to help you land a brilliant deal:

### MOT Status is Key

As mentioned, an MOT certificate is a crucial indicator of a car's roadworthiness. It ensures key components like brakes, tyres, and lights are in working order. Always check when the MOT expires, and if it's due soon, factor that into your offer. Find out more about what an MOT covers in [our handy MOT guide](/our-mot-guide).

### Service History & V5C

Always ask to see the car's service history and the V5C logbook. The V5C proves ownership, and the service history gives you an insight into how well the car has been maintained. Regular servicing is a good sign, even for older, cheaper cars.

### A Good Inspection (and Test Drive!)

Don't be shy! Give the car a thorough once-over. Check for rust, listen for unusual noises during the test drive, and make sure all the electrics work. If you're not confident, bring a mechanically-minded friend or even a professional along. It’s better to be safe than sorry!
